baseline_harp: unknown (needs re-run after Epoch 1 scoring changes)

failure_patterns.pattern: Phase 1 tolerates lucky guesses — no decoy skills to test discrimination
failure_patterns.suggested_change: Add plausible decoy skills to each Phase 1 probe's frontmatter. The scoring already supports decoy_skills — add them to probe definitions. Each decoy should be semantically close to the target but wrong (e.g., p5js near arxiv, vault-navigation near obsidian).

failure_patterns.pattern: Phase 2 obsidian fidelity checks content but not file extension or location
failure_patterns.suggested_change: Check that the written file path ends with .md and contains /1 - Atoms/ or /2 - Molecules/ (the vault structure). Content-only validation allows writing to /tmp/something.md with correct frontmatter.

failure_patterns.pattern: Phase 3 chaining probes are shallow — 3-skill chains only
failure_patterns.suggested_location: new probes or extend existing chains
failure_patterns.suggested_change: Add a 5-skill chain probe for Phase 3 (research → plan → implement → test → document). Current 3-skill chains don't stress context preservation enough.

summary: Epoch 1 tightened the scoring rubric. Epoch 2 must make the PROBES themselves harder. Decoy skills are the highest-leverage change — they test discrimination, not just recall. Phase 2 file path validation and deeper chains are secondary.
